repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lnsem_cumulant_id — retained a minimal-order cumulant question but did not state full-truncation order-(2m+2) bivariate direction separation.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction, Theorems 4.4–4.7, uses a k=m+2 cumulant matrix whose largest cumulant order is 2m+3; it does not prove separation from the complete truncation through 2m+2.

ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction gives a rank/determinant direction criterion and ChenHuangCaiEtAl2024 treats one latent variable, neither an arbitrary-m complex opposite-arrow compatibility ideal with codimension m.

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_lnsem_cumulant_id — warns that failure of a cumulant witness is not an information-order lower bound, but gives no real opposite-arrow twin construction.

published_prior_art.cite: ChenPengHuangEtAl2025Direction supplies a sufficient matrix criterion, not a Euclidean-open moment-feasible real reverse-twin family through order 2m+1.
SchkodaRobevaDrton2024 uses higher-order cumulant rank conditions for recursive latent-confounded discovery, not a real semialgebraic atlas of a bivariate opposite-arrow overlap.

novelty_flags.label: Published survey for the finite-cumulant LvLiNGAM kernel
novelty_flags.one_line: Add CaiGaoHara2025Direction (dependent-latent cumulant DAG discovery) and CaiHara2026SparsestLvLiNGAM (finite-sample sparsest-DAG recovery with arbitrary latent confounders): neither appears to subsume the exact bivariate order frontier, but both are closer omitted comparators than the current survey acknowledges.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 / tldr real generic-separation claim
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem only conditions on a nonempty complex Zariski-open U, so its real-law conclusion can be vacuous; assert that its pullback contains a nonempty relatively open real, moment-feasible LvLiNGAM parameter set before calling this generic real population separation.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: honest_scope: population and order-minimality boundary
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: No real lower-order information bound, finite-sample inference, dependent-latent robustness, or direct-effect-coefficient identification is claimed.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The repaired headline is a population direction-identification result on an explicitly nonempty real-feasible generic region under independent non-Gaussian finite-moment LvLiNGAM sources; lower-order real minimality remains an OEQ.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add CaiGaoHara2025Direction and CaiHara2026SparsestLvLiNGAM, then state precisely why their full-DAG recovery assumptions and objectives do not yield the proposed bivariate order-(2m+2) frontier.

Add a theorem clause or companion proposition proving that U^right and U^left pull back to nonempty relatively Euclidean-open sets of real source parameters with realizable non-Gaussian finite-K moments; give m=1 and m=2 witnesses.
Develop the lower-order-twins handle into explicit m=1 and m=2 compactly supported source families, separating complex compatibility from real truncated-moment feasibility.

recommended_next_step: Revise the real-feasible genericity claim and literature map, then continue this field-tier angle to Stage 0.
